name: Maguro Bluefin Tuna Japan
desc: The king of sushi. Known for various cuts like Akami, Chutoro, and the fatty Otoro, it is the most prized fish in markets across Japan.

name: Sake Atlantic Salmon Japan
desc: Incredibly popular for its rich flavor and melt-in-the-mouth texture. A staple topping for sushi and grilled breakfast sets in Japan.

name: Madako Common Octopus Japan
desc: Essential for Takoyaki and Sashimi platters. Often blanched to bring out its sweet flavor and firm texture in the kitchens of Japan.

name: Surume Ika Squid Japan
desc: Highly versatile seafood used for Sashimi, Tempura, or dried as a salty snack (Atarime) to enjoy with drinks in Japan.

name: Kuruma Ebi Tiger Prawn Japan
desc: A luxury seafood item prized for its sweetness and beautiful striped pattern. Often served as high-end Tempura or Nigiri in Japan.

name: Zuwaigani Snow Crab Japan
desc: A winter delicacy, particularly in the Sea of Japan coastal regions. Known for its sweet, delicate leg meat and rich innards in Japan.

name: Fugu Pufferfish Delicacy Japan
desc: A famous and daring delicacy that requires a licensed chef to prepare. It is often served as paper-thin sashimi slices in Japan.

name: Hotate Scallop Shellfish Japan
desc: Plump and sweet scallops, with the best quality typically harvested from the cold waters of Hokkaido in Northern Japan.

name: Uni Sea Urchin Japan
desc: Creamy, briny, and rich roe that is considered a luxury ingredient for 'Gunkan' sushi and seafood bowls in Japan.

name: Ikura Salmon Roe Japan
desc: Large, translucent orange pearls of salmon roe that burst with salty flavor. A favorite topping for Chirashi-zushi in Japan.

name: Unagi Freshwater Eel Japan
desc: Grilled with a sweet soy-based glaze (Kabayaki). Traditionally eaten during the 'Midsummer Day of the Ox' for stamina in Japan.

name: Tai Sea Bream Japan
desc: Associated with the word 'medetai' (auspicious). This red fish is served at weddings and New Year celebrations as a symbol of luck in Japan.
